title: Molten Malaysian Chicken Liver Toast
description: A comforting, slightly bitter toast topped with sautéed chicken liver and a molten sweet‑savory sauce, served in a traditional copper mug.
instructions: Heat the butter in a skillet over medium heat until melted.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chicken liver to the skillet, season with salt and pepper, and cook until browned on all sides, about 3 minutes.
Stir in fish sauce, soy sauce, tamarind paste, sugar, lime juice, and chili flakes, then pour in the coconut milk and cook until the sauce thickens slightly, about 2 minutes.
Toast the bread slices in a separate pan until golden.
Place each toasted slice on a clean plate, then spoon the molten sauce over the top so it pools around the edges.
Transfer the sauced toast into a preheated copper mug, letting the sauce flow under the bread to create a molten effect.
Serve immediately while the sauce is still warm, pairing with a light salad or cucumber slices if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
